claimText: Ocean darkening refers to the reduction in light penetration into the oceans, which shrinks the photic zone (the sunlit layer up to 200 meters deep where 90% of marine life thrives).
evidenceExcerpt: Ocean darkening shrinks the photic zone, which is the sunlit layer up to 200 meters deep where 90% of marine life thrives.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition of ocean darkening and the depth/significance of the photic zone.
distractorHooks: Expanding the photic zone instead of shrinking it
Stating the photic zone extends up to 500 or 1000 meters deep
Claiming the aphotic zone is where 90% of marine life thrives

claimText: Coastal ocean darkening is primarily caused by nutrient, organic material, and sediment loading from agricultural runoff, whereas open ocean darkening is driven by surface warming that leads to algal blooms.
evidenceExcerpt: Coastal darkening is caused by nutrient, organic material, and sediment loading from agricultural runoff. Open ocean darkening is driven by surface warming leading to algal blooms and climate-driven circulation changes.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question distinguishing the specific drivers of coastal versus open ocean darkening.
distractorHooks: Swapping the causes (e.g., stating sediment loading is the primary cause of open ocean darkening)
Claiming surface cooling causes algal blooms in the open ocean

claimText: Between 2003 and 2022, 21% of the global ocean became darker, with notable occurrences in the Arctic, Antarctic, and Gulf Stream regions.
evidenceExcerpt: 21% of the global ocean became darker between 2003 and 2022, notably in the Arctic, Antarctic, and Gulf Stream.
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical regions most affected by recent ocean darkening trends.
distractorHooks: Claiming ocean darkening is exclusively a tropical or equatorial phenomenon
Stating that 100% of the global ocean has become darker

claimText: The impacts of global ocean darkening include limited ocean productivity, declining fish stocks, and disruptions to climate regulation.
evidenceExcerpt: Impacts include limited ocean productivity, declining fish stocks, and affected climate regulation.
examUse: Multi-statement question evaluating the ecological and environmental consequences of ocean darkening.
distractorHooks: Claiming ocean darkening increases overall marine productivity
Stating that ocean darkening has no impact on global climate regulation
